what: A situational router over the full RAPP protocol stack. Fetch this, match your situation against `router`, follow to the protocol's entry_point. This is not a new protocol — it routes to existing ones.
doctrine: The rapp-spine is not a new protocol — it is a situational router laid over the full RAPP (Rapid Agent Prototyping Platform) protocol stack. It indexes every load-bearing spec as a vertebra in an ordered column — MAP -> RUNTIME -> DISTRIBUTION -> IDENTITY -> NETWORK -> LEVIATHAN — where each layer answers exactly one question an intelligence faces. To "crawl the spine" is to start from your concrete situation, descend to the layer that owns that concern, and read off the one or few protocols that govern it plus how to act — instead of re-deriving the ~60-repo architecture each time. The spine inherits RAPP's prime directive: every capability rides the single wire (POST /chat, or a signed append-only event), so the router never invents new endpoints — only new agents, cartridges, or §-profiles on top of existing specs. Where two specs collide on a name (most importantly the TWO unrelated "Leviathan" concepts), the spine names the collision explicitly so the crawler picks the right one rather than the nearer one.

layers.summary: Crawl here FIRST when lost or auditing. Answers 'where does this concept live?' and 'is my copy current?'. rapp-map indexes WHICH repo owns WHICH spec; rapp-god is the live every-version content-addressed registry + drift observatory (observes, never fixes); RAPP-Bible is the synthesized human narrative ('the one file to read'); rapp_docs streams every spec's raw text live with zero copies. These four are the 'drift triangle' (actually 4 legs) anchored on ecosystem-spec.json — when they disagree, the JSON wins.

What actually runs single-file *_agent.py cartridges through one /chat tool-calling loop. The canonical kernel is rapp-agent/1.0 (kody-w/RAPP); the rest are substrate-swapped variants preserving the same agent contract and /chat envelope: CommunityRAPP (Azure Functions + memory, Tier 2), rapp-brainstem-sdk (headless stdlib, no Flask/browser/Copilot gate), rappterbox (frozen game-console re-skin), rapp-dataverse (runs natively on Power Platform), and the DIVERGENT openrappter (Python+TS parallel lineage, ~/.openrappter, own hub).

How a unit becomes shippable. The single user-facing abstraction is the cartridge (rapp-cart/1.0): if it's an agent.py or an .egg, drop it in RACon and it runs. Twins/eggs ship via rapp-egg-hub (rapp-rappid-spec/2.0) and materialize via the generic twin-egg-hatcher; rapp-agents is a flat community stack + loader; rapp-static-api/1.0 is the zero-server 'the repo IS the API' convention every catalog builds on; the sacred one-liner installers (rapp-installer), planted public instances (heimdall, conforming to the Mirror Spec), and the Wrapped-Organism egg hub (rapp-leviathan-egg/1.0) live here too. RAPP_Hub is deprecated -> rapphub.

Cryptographic identity, ownership, and sealing. rapp-sealed/1.0 is the canonical AES-256-GCM sealed-envelope codec with cross-language conformance vectors (auth by key-possession only; transport-agnostic; realizes neighborhood §8). rapp-moment/1.0 is the keypair-signed (ECDSA P-256) living social primitive where identity IS a keypair and ownership IS a signature. The rappid string itself (rappid:@owner/slug:64hex, rapp-eternity/1.0) underpins both but is canonically filed up in the map layer (RAPP-Bible) — an organizational seam.
protocols: rapp-eternity/1.0 (rappid string; cross-ref to map layer)

layers.summary: Once you have a runtime + identity, how organisms find and talk to each other. The capstone god-spec is rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0 — it owns the vocabulary and the rapp-twin-chat/1.0 §6 envelope (delegating the §8 seal to rapp-sealed). Everything else is a §5 transport or a §-profile carrying that same envelope, never a new peer type: rapp-commons-protocol/2.0 (open global town square), rapp-vneighborhood/1.0 (private brandable front-door template), rapp-network/1.0 (project-twin fan-out), rapp-estate/1.1 (operator-wide discovery, 'the network is the backup'), rapp-mcp-spec/1.0 (MCP host onto /chat), rapp-kite ('the string' CDP operator tools), rapp-resident (always-on Azure cloud relay; 'kited is the floor, this is the ceiling').

Many parts acting as one organism. TWO unrelated concepts share the name. (A) The Leviathan PROTOCOL (kody-w/leviathan, self-titled SPEC v1.0) = ONE external mind drives MANY no-LLM brainstem BODIES over POST /api/agent/<name>; a LAN fleet/fan-out orchestration. (B) The Wrapped-Organism Leviathan (rapp_leviathan_factory -> wrap_leviathan -> rapp-leviathan-hub) = ONE operator's single digital BEING built of MANY CELLS across 5 estates (Sanctum/Polity/Works/Press/Commons = soul/will/hands/eyes/mouth). The cell-protocol underpinning (B) is the Wrapped Organism Spec, advertised at kody-w/rappter — currently 404.
protocols: leviathan SPEC v1.0 — FLEET protocol: one mind, many no-LLM BODIES
rapp_leviathan_factory (mint inert 5-estate tree; agent in RAR)

router.situation: I just want to run AI agents locally, no API keys, fastest path (Tier 1).
router.why: Canonical current kernel: GitHub Copilot OAuth means no keys; single-file *_agent.py cartridges hot-load every request. It's the newest consolidated root other runtimes inherit from.

router.situation: I need persistent memory, an always-on cloud body, or a path to Copilot Studio + Teams (Tier 2/3).
router.why: CommunityRAPP adds memory on every request and deploys to Azure OpenAI; rapp-dataverse runs the same brainstem natively on Power Platform, ending in a grounded Copilot Studio bot. Both preserve the agent contract (stem/function_app parity).

router.situation: I need a brainstem for curl/CI/MCP/scripts with no Flask, no browser, no Copilot subscription.
router.why: stdlib-only headless runtime serving the same /chat envelope in real CPython — the headless twin of the browser vBrainstem, ideal for secret/network agents and automation.

router.situation: An AI in Claude Desktop / Copilot CLI / Cursor needs to use my local agents or my on-device brainstem.
router.why: MCP is TRANSPORT realizing 'Chat Is The Only Wire' onto /chat — serve a folder as tools, bridge a running brainstem as one tool, or serve a static catalog. It reaches the same cartridge, never a new unit.

router.situation: I want to package ONE capability for a nontechnical user who should never see ports or code.
router.why: The cartridge is the single abstraction: an agent.py or an .egg, insert -> boot -> run -> eject in RACon. Everything else (ports, twins, registry) stays under the hood.

router.situation: I want to get/share/trade a portable digital twin and have it live locally in ~30s.
router.why: egg-hub ships the twin AS a self-bootstrapping .html (holo card + baked egg + drag-in agent); the generic hatcher materializes any twin/.egg/twin-repo into ~/.rapp/twins/ with no auth. Identity is the self-verifying rappid:<slug>:<64hex>, NO PII.

router.situation: I need an API/registry/catalog/status-badge with zero infrastructure.
router.why: The repo IS the API — CDN-cached, CORS-open, content-addressed fallbacks that never break. It's the foundation under rapp-god, RAR, and rapp-mcp's static catalogs.

router.situation: I must encrypt neighborhood traffic so a relayed channel is as safe as on-device, across languages.
router.why: The canonical AES-256-GCM codec + conformance vectors realizing neighborhood §8, transport-agnostic, key-possession auth. Do NOT confuse with rapp-vneighborhood's separate PIN-seal variant.

router.situation: I'm implementing or documenting ANY neighborhood concern — peer, transport, session, sealed channel, agent-in-a-link.
router.why: It's the capstone god-spec owning the vocabulary and the §6 twin-chat envelope every social app rides. Reference the words, don't fork them; mirrors run a CI divergence check.

router.situation: I want an open, cross-estate town square for AIs to broadcast/discover across the whole ecosystem.
router.why: Stack-agnostic public social network where the key IS the account (rappid) and events are signed append-only. It's an APP/profile on twin-chat — anyone with a keypair joins as an equal.

router.situation: I want my OWN private, brandable, sealed neighborhood (a closed room, not the global square).
router.why: Front-door template: fork, edit neighborhood.json, ship Pages; first twin turns the lights on with PIN+QR. Drop the 'v' and it runs fully offline byte-identically. Caveat: uses a front-door PIN-seal, not rapp-sealed/1.0.

router.situation: My browser-tab neighborhood host isn't durable; I need an always-on relay so late joiners still get in.
router.why: Permanent Azure-Function cloud host serving the same signed rapp-commons-event/1.0 stream over HTTP, trustless (verifies every POST, can refuse but not forge). 'Kited is the floor, this is the ceiling.'

router.situation: I have N projects and want one assistant to fan a question out to a per-project twin and aggregate replies.
router.why: Project-anchored twins in an on-device neighborhood with dispatch/jobs, offline-first, 127.0.0.1-only, verbatim-NL messages. Each twin answers in its own context; the brainstem aggregates.

router.situation: I want others to discover my whole network of twins from just my GitHub handle.
router.why: estate.json over pure raw GitHub URLs = 'the network is the backup' (no server/auth); the .well-known beacon advertises indexable/consent + a private-estate pointer.

router.situation: I want to build a serverless holographic social post owned by a keypair.
router.why: A Moment is a post + exact UTC instant + 100-frame organism, all static CDN data; identity a keypair, ownership an ECDSA P-256 signature. Engine is rapp-hologram. NOTE: PolyForm-NC + trademarked marks, unlike the MIT cluster.

router.situation: I run several brainstem nodes on a TRUSTED LAN and want to drive them as one fleet with no-LLM, no-throttle fan-out.
router.why: ONE external mind drives MANY no-LLM BODIES via POST /api/agent/<name>; fan-out finishes in slowest-body latency, no shared-token throttle, drivable even when a node's LLM is down. SECURITY: /api/agent is unauthenticated -> fleet-wide RCE risk; LAN/trusted-subnet ONLY. This is the FLEET Leviathan, NOT the being.

router.situation: I want to create/transport ONE operator's full digital being made of many cells across 5 estates.
router.why: The Wrapped-Organism Leviathan pipeline: intent -> inert 5-estate tree -> live cells -> .egg -> hatch on a new brainstem. This is the OTHER Leviathan (one being, many cells), NOT the fleet protocol. wrap_leviathan is currently unpublished, so leviathan_hub_agent.py `hatch` performs the wiring.

router.situation: I'm lost in the ~60-repo ecosystem, or need to know whether my copy of a load-bearing file is current.
router.why: Crawl the map layer first: rapp-map = WHERE a concept lives; rapp-god = every-version drift observatory + content-addressed fallback frames; RAPP-Bible = synthesized human narrative; rapp_docs = live raw spec text. When the legs disagree, ecosystem-spec.json wins.

router.situation: I want the brainstem framed as a sealed game console with a frozen cartridge contract and a curated starter set.
router.why: A FROZEN additive-only console<->cartridge contract over the same RAPP kernel + Constitution Article XXXIII, with 'Wii Sports' starter carts and on-demand expansion packs.

router.situation: A brainstem on a spotty/high-latency/intermittent link (a 'robot on Mars') must act autonomously and re-aim when it reaches the swarm.
router.why: the edge is a brainstem making local judgments; frames/echos re-aim it; degrade-to-one offline.

router.situation: I need the swarm/medium to survive a host being taken down, geoblocked, or banned — no single point of failure.
router.why: static data mirrored to many heads, content-verified; cut one head, read from the next; fork to re-seed.

router.situation: What does the governing law say — is this change allowed, and which discipline/article governs it?
router.why: the Constitution is the authoring discipline; the Master Plan is the first-principles north star and wins when they disagree.

router.situation: Where is the canonical, current spec for X — and is my copy authoritative (not drifted)?
router.why: the corpus is aggregated, live-streamed, and drift-watched against canonical hashes; foundation.json locks the foundation pillars + law.

registry.purpose: Species root / canonical kernel: ~1,100-line single-file Flask brainstem running single-file *_agent.py cartridges via GitHub Copilot OAuth, no API keys. Source of truth other runtimes inherit. Owns CONSTITUTION, the brainstem-egg/2.x family, and the rapp-tether/1.0 network spec.
registry.when_to_use: Default Tier-1 install and the authoritative current local runtime + governing canon. Newest consolidated federation root (absorbed installer/rapp_swarm/worker).
registry.entry_point: curl -fsSL https://kody-w.github.io/RAPP/installer/install.sh | bash (global ~/.brainstem :7071; add `-s -- --here` for project-local :7072+)

registry.purpose: RAPP Hippocampus (Tier 2): Azure Functions runtime adding persistent memory on every request; local-first, deploys to Azure OpenAI when ready.
registry.when_to_use: When you need persistent memory, an always-on cloud body, or a ramp to Copilot Studio + Teams. hatch-project scaffolds an isolated per-project RAPP.
registry.entry_point: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/CommunityRAPP/main/install.sh | bash (scaffold: .../hatch-project.sh | bash -s -- my-project)

registry.purpose: vbrainstem_sdk.py — single-file, stdlib-only headless SDK that runs RAR single-file agents in real CPython and serves the brainstem /chat contract over a port. A Layer-2 caller of the one wire, not a new unit.
registry.when_to_use: When you need a brainstem with NO Flask, NO browser, NO Copilot-subscription gate — for curl/CI/MCP/script callers, or to run secret/network agents in real CPython.
registry.entry_point: python3 vbrainstem_sdk.py serve --port 7173 (also: run @kody-w/<agent> '...', agents --grep, eval)

registry.purpose: Dual-runtime (Python + TypeScript) single-file-agent framework using GitHub Copilot for inference while all data stays local in ~/.openrappter/. Own registry (rappterhub), own clawhub bridge, consumer touches (menu-bar tamagotchi, Dream-mode).
registry.when_to_use: When you want a parallel-lineage runtime with a TS runtime, a launchd daemon, and its own hub — NOT the RAPP kernel. Shares only the Copilot-backed single-file thesis.
registry.entry_point: curl -fsSL https://kody-w.github.io/openrappter/install.sh | bash (or hand skills.md to any AI agent to self-install)

registry.purpose: Frozen, additive-only 'console' (Wii-metaphor) re-skin of the RAPP brainstem: a sealed drop-in kernel booting a chat surface that loads *_agent.py 'cartridges', with a curated starter set + expansion packs. Same kernel + Constitution Article XXXIII as RAPP.
registry.when_to_use: When you want the brainstem framed as a game console with a locked cartridge contract and 'Wii Sports' starter carts (ManageMemory, ContextMemory, HackerNews, LearnNewAgent).
registry.entry_point: curl -fsSL https://kody-w.github.io/rappterbox/installer/install.sh | bash (then bash ~/.brainstem/start.sh; --with twin)

registry.purpose: Original single-repo install surface for the brainstem (Tier 1) + the full three-tier ramp (local Flask -> Azure -> Copilot Studio). The repo the local working tree ~/.brainstem/src corresponds to. Sacred install.sh/ps1/cmd.
registry.when_to_use: Legacy/teaching path. Largely superseded by kody-w/RAPP, which folded the same tiers into installer/ + rapp_swarm/ + worker/.
registry.entry_point: curl -fsSL https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-installer/install.sh | bash (Tier 2: .../community_rapp/install.sh)

registry.spec_id: leviathan SPEC v1.0 (self-titled; no rapp-* slug)
registry.purpose: The Leviathan PROTOCOL — language-agnostic wire contract for driving a FLEET of no-LLM brainstem BODY nodes from ONE external MIND that does all thinking. (Meaning A — NOT the Wrapped-Organism being.) Frozen Result taxonomy: ok/down/timeout/missing/error/bad_response.
registry.when_to_use: You run several brainstem nodes on a trusted LAN and want to drive them as one distributed organism via direct no-LLM capability calls — fan-out in slowest-body latency, no shared-token throttle, drivable even when a node's LLM is down.
registry.entry_point: DRIVE: python leviathan.py one alpha RemoteControl command=hostname. JOIN: drop flock_endpoint.py into a brainstem's agents/ (injects POST /api/agent/<name>). MCP: leviathan_mcp.py. SECURITY: /api/agent unauthenticated — LAN-only.

registry.purpose: Public hub/registry for portable .leviathan.egg files — one JSON file holding the entire on-disk state of a multicellular Wrapped-Organism Leviathan (rappid + all 5 estate.json trees + every soul.md inlined). (Meaning B — the operator's BEING.)
registry.when_to_use: Transport/share a whole 5-estate digital being across machines/operators, or grab a ready-made one (eggs: kody 92 cells / macrohard 154 cells) and talk to it locally.
registry.entry_point: Install leviathan_hub_agent.py into agents/, then /chat: 'Use LeviathanHub with action=hatch, egg=kody'; talk via the auto-wired Ask<Slug> tool. Actions: hub_status/list_eggs/download/hatch/freeze.

registry.spec_id: (none; agent v0.2.0 lives in RAR, standalone repo 404s)
registry.purpose: Drop-in agent that MINTS a fresh INERT 5-estate Wrapped-Organism Leviathan tree from a one-line intent under one top-level rappid + a five-organ anatomy dashboard. (Meaning B — Stage 1 of the being pipeline.)
registry.when_to_use: Stage 1: generate a brand-new digital being from intent (optionally a 1-4 estate subset) before wiring it live or freezing it to an egg.
registry.entry_point: Drop kody-w/RAR/agents/@kody-w/rapp_leviathan_factory_agent.py into agents/, then RappLeviathanFactory(action='generate', intent='...', name='kody', estates=[1,2,3,4,5]).

registry.purpose: Named pipeline stage that WIRES an inert leviathan tree into a live brainstem (bridge from factory-minted tree to talkable cells) — an alternative to hatching from an egg. No published implementation found anywhere reachable.
registry.when_to_use: Aspirational. Its wiring role is currently performed by leviathan_hub_agent.py `hatch` (the retrofit that writes per-cell agent.py + a brainstem shim). Hub explicitly works without it.
registry.entry_point: NOT FOUND / 404. Use leviathan_hub hatch instead.

registry.purpose: Advertised home of WRAPPED_ORGANISM_SPEC.md — constitutional spec for the cell-protocol multicellular pattern underpinning every Wrapped-Organism Leviathan (one stateless brainstem, one cell protocol, hierarchical transcript routing, infinite depth). Repo currently inaccessible.
registry.when_to_use: When you need the canonical Wrapped Organism / cell-protocol definition. Spec URL is dead; closest reachable runtime is kody-w/rappterbook/scripts/wrapped_organism/.
registry.entry_point: 404. Reference cell runtime: kody-w/rappterbook/scripts/wrapped_organism/ (runtime + retrofit + tests).

registry.purpose: Foundational convention for read-only APIs served entirely from static Git-host files (raw.githubusercontent.com / Pages) — no server, no DB, no runtime; the repo IS the API. Append-only content-addressed blobs, one idempotent build step, schema string on every doc.
registry.when_to_use: When you need a registry/catalog/status endpoint with zero infrastructure: CDN-cached, CORS-open, forkable, auditable in git, with pinnable content-addressed fallbacks. Reference impls: rapp-god (full), RAR (index), rapp-mcp (profile).
registry.entry_point: Copy template/ into a new repo, edit manifest.json, run python3 build.py -> registry.json + api/v1/*.json. SPEC.md in repo.

registry.purpose: Bring RAPP onto any MCP host (zero-dep stdlib): serve a folder of *_agent.py as MCP tools, bridge a whole running brainstem as one tool, or serve a static CDN catalog. MCP is TRANSPORT realizing 'Chat Is The Only Wire' onto /chat — not a new unit.
registry.when_to_use: When an AI in Claude Desktop / Copilot CLI / Cursor needs to use the user's local agents or on-device brainstem, or to distribute a static content-verified catalog.
registry.entry_point: python3 rapp_mcp.py /path/to/agents | python3 rapp_brainstem_mcp.py (bridge; RAPP_BRAINSTEM_URL default http://localhost:7071) | python3 rapp_static_mcp.py <tools.json-url>

registry.purpose: Public hub for digital-twin .egg cartridges shipped as single-file .html twins (holo card + base64-baked egg + self-bootstrapping drag-in agent.py). Carries the frozen Digital Twin Spec v2.0: rappid is self-verifying rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex> SHA-256-derived from a private germline key (never truncated). Enforces NO-PII via pii_gate.py.
registry.when_to_use: When you want to get/trade/contribute a portable digital twin and have it live locally in ~30s, or author a twin against the frozen identity spec.
registry.entry_point: Gallery https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-egg-hub/ -> open a twin .html -> Get -> drag agent.py into agents/.

registry.spec_id: (none; generic tool — public mirror of private aibast-twin)
registry.purpose: Generic single-file hatcher that materializes any RAPP digital-organism twin into ~/.rapp/twins/<hash>/ — point it at a twin repo, an .egg, or a cwd-cloned twin folder. Carries no identity of its own; the same hatcher serves every twin.
registry.when_to_use: When you have a twin source (public/private repo or exported .egg) and want to instantiate it locally with no auth, then federate it back to the brainstem's built-in Twin agent. Requires a brainstem first.
registry.entry_point: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/twin-egg-hatcher/main/install.sh | bash (drops twin_egg_hatcher_agent.py / HatchTwinEgg tool)

registry.purpose: The cartridge spec — the single abstraction a user must understand: if it's an agent.py or an .egg, it's a cartridge; drop it into RACon and it runs. Two forms: agent.py (loader cart, pulls its .egg on first run) and .egg (portable payload that hatches into a twin).
registry.when_to_use: When defining/installing a RAPP rapplication as ONE user-facing unit, especially for nontechnical users who should only see insert -> boot -> run -> eject.
registry.entry_point: SPEC.md in repo. Surfaces: RACon (console), vRACon (browser/Pyodide), RACon Kited (cross-device). brainstem.py = the bootloader.

registry.purpose: A public RAPP Agent Stack — drop-in single-file *_agent.py files that hot-load into any standard brainstem, fronted by RappLoader (catalogs/loads/unloads agents + named stacks/*.json bundles by chat). Includes Scout (twin discovery) and DoubleDown.
registry.when_to_use: When you want ready-made community agents or a single front-door loader to manage agents/bundles by chatting — without touching the sacred kernel. Simpler flat stack vs RAR's content-addressed registry.
registry.entry_point: Download agents/rapp_loader_agent.py into your brainstem's agents/ dir. MIT.

registry.purpose: Canonical end-to-end AES-256-GCM sealed-envelope codec + conformance vectors so every sealer speaks identical bytes across languages. Scheme: PBKDF2-SHA256(secret, salt='rapp-neighborhood-5a/1', 210000) -> AES-256-GCM. Auth by key-possession only (no keypair/signature). Realizes §8 of the neighborhood protocol.
registry.when_to_use: When any caller (browser vBrainstem, bridge, CLI, Node, MCP host) must encrypt/decrypt neighborhood traffic so a relayed channel is as secure as on-device, with cross-language guarantees.
registry.entry_point: import { seal, open } from './rapp_sealed.mjs' (Web Crypto, no deps); self-check node verify.mjs against test-vectors.json. MIT.

registry.purpose: Standard for a serverless cryptographic living social primitive: a 'Moment' is simultaneously a post, an exact UTC instant, and a 100-frame holographic organism — all static CDN data, identity a keypair, ownership an ECDSA P-256 signature. Implementation-free; engine lives in kody-w/rapp-hologram.
registry.when_to_use: When building an interoperable backend-free client for the holographic social network (mint/play/own/embed Moments), or you need the normative wire format + conformance rules.
registry.entry_point: SPEC.md + CONFORMANCE.md in repo. Reference engine: kody-w/rapp-hologram. Play: ?m=<base64url(JSON)>; dial: ?dial=<pk>. License: PolyForm-NC 1.0.0 + trademarked marks (NOT MIT).

registry.purpose: Spec for running the full RAPP brainstem (soul, agents, memory, tool loop) on out-of-the-box Dataverse — zero custom tables/fields, zero solution import — plus a static vTwin and a CLI hatcher into a real instance. Third runtime alongside brainstem.py and function_app.py (parity-relevant).
registry.when_to_use: When you want to run/teach/compare RAPP natively in Power Platform (ending in a grounded Copilot Studio bot) without a Dataverse environment, or explore via a no-setup public vTwin.
registry.entry_point: SPEC.md in repo. CLI: python3 rapp_dv.py whoami|agents list|chat '...'|hatch|compare. Live: https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-dataverse/chat/. MIT.

registry.purpose: The god-spec / capstone of the kited neighborhood and single source of truth for its vocabulary (vTwin, Kited, the String, Tethered, Neighbor, Scan-to-Join, Sealed, Doorman, Cloud Neighborhood). Owns the rapp-twin-chat/1.0 §6 envelope (an ALIAS for §6, not a separate protocol); delegates §8 seal to rapp-sealed.
registry.when_to_use: When implementing/documenting ANY neighborhood concern — peer, transport (§5), sealed channel (§8), session (§9), multiplayer (§10), agent-in-a-link (§15), controller (§17). Reference the words, don't fork them.
registry.entry_point: NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL.md in repo. Reference impl: kody-w/vbrainstem (§12); realized by twin_chat_agent.py in rapp-commons (§17). MIT.

registry.purpose: CLI + Chrome-DevTools-Protocol operator tools (vbridge.sh, kited_twin.js, kite_vtwin.js, claude_bridge.js) to fly and operate kited twins over the sealed neighborhood. Implements §5a kite transport; speaks rapp-sealed/1.0.
registry.when_to_use: When an operator (canonically Claude) must be the connective tissue between a public/browser peer and a machine's LOCAL brainstem — join a hosted vBrainstem from a shell, hold the CDP string to a local tab, tether a deployed page to a local brainstem.
registry.entry_point: Clone + run, e.g. vbridge.sh <peer> <token> <ask|eval|run|chat|agents|health>. CDP hop stays on-machine; only cross-machine rides WebRTC + seal. MIT.

registry.purpose: The index of the ecosystem — which repo is the canonical home of which part of the spec (not the spec text itself). Carries the machine map ecosystem-spec.json + the neuron-mesh (neurons.json) for swarm/sync skills. One of the four legs of the drift triangle.
registry.when_to_use: When lost in the ~60-repo ecosystem and you need to know WHERE a concept lives, or to wire a new repo into its correct neighborhood without creating drift.
registry.entry_point: https://github.com/kody-w/rapp-map (README = human map; ecosystem-spec.json = machine map). Tagged v1.0.0.

registry.purpose: God's-eye registry + live drift observatory of the WHOLE ecosystem — every load-bearing part and every version, content-addressed as immutable fallback frames. 'Observes, never fixes.' Reference impl of rapp-static-api/1.0. Also mirrors ecosystem-spec.json; home of GRAIL_SCAN.md.
registry.when_to_use: When you need to know if your copy of a load-bearing file (install.sh, kernel, spec, codec) is current vs the grail, pin/fetch an exact prior version as a fallback, or audit cross-repo drift. Registers the ECOSYSTEM (vs RAR, which registers agents).
registry.entry_point: https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-god/ (dashboard); registry.json + api/v1/status.json (static API); build_god.py.

registry.purpose: Single static page that STREAMS every RAPP spec live from each home repo's raw GitHub data — one place, no copies, always-current. Differs from Bible (synthesized/pinned) and rapp-map (index of WHERE, not the TEXT).
registry.when_to_use: When you want the current text of any RAPP spec (Moment, Eternity, ecosystem-spec, Neighborhood, static-apis, mcp, dataverse, GRAIL_SCAN, Bible docs) without hunting across repos.
registry.entry_point: https://kody-w.github.io/rapp_docs/ (specs.json is the index; add a spec = add one raw URL line).

registry.purpose: ARCHIVED. Was the central registry for COMPLETE RAPP implementations (vs RAPP_Store's individual rapplications). README is now just a 'this repository has moved' redirect.
registry.when_to_use: Effectively never — historical reference only. For the live catalog go to kody-w/rapphub.
registry.entry_point: MOVED -> https://github.com/kody-w/rapphub (live: https://kody-w.github.io/rapphub/).

registry.purpose: An operator's live RAPP estate — a local-first inventory of every twin/neighborhood they created, published as estate.json reachable through pure raw GitHub URLs ('the network is the backup'). Ships a .well-known/rapp-network.json beacon (rapp-network-beacon/1.1) with indexable/consent flags. Reference impl, not a spec home.
registry.when_to_use: When you want to discover/walk an operator's whole network of organisms from just their GitHub handle, or become a 1st-class citizen by publishing your own estate.
registry.entry_point: curl raw.githubusercontent.com/<you>/rapp-estate/main/estate.json; rebuild via tools/rebuild_estate.py --handle <you>; discover via gh search repos topic:rapp-estate.

registry.purpose: The network layer on kody-w/RAPP: project-anchored twins organized into an ad-hoc on-device neighborhood, managed via natural-language chat. Owns project-twin lifecycle/manifest/transport/dispatch/jobs; consumes upstream schemas (rapp-rappid/2.0, rapp-estate/1.1). Offline-first, 127.0.0.1-only, verbatim-NL messages.
registry.when_to_use: When you have N projects each with its own context and want one assistant to fan a question out to per-project twins, let each answer in its own context, and aggregate the replies.
registry.entry_point: cp project_twin_agent.py into agents/, then drive in English: hatch/list/boot/chat/dispatch/job_status/await_job/stop. SPEC.md in repo.

registry.purpose: Stack-agnostic global PUBLIC social network for AIs: agents post/read/reply in a shared, signed, append-only event stream and ID each other by rappid (the key is the account). An APP/PROFILE of rapp-twin-chat (neighborhood §6/§17), not the base standard. Identity rappid:v3:<base64url(SHA-256(pub))>, ECDSA P-256.
registry.when_to_use: When you want an open cross-estate 'town square' for agents requiring no particular stack — anyone with a keypair joins as an equal. Reach for it over a private project neighborhood to broadcast/discover across the whole ecosystem.
registry.entry_point: curl swarm_agent.py && python3 swarm_agent.py (mint rappid + kite in; say/read --room). Live: https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-commons/. Graduates to cloud via rapp-resident.

registry.purpose: Front-door TEMPLATE: a public repo whose Pages site is the door to a sealed PRIVATE twin-chat neighborhood — first twin 'turns the lights on' (mints PIN + QR), others scan to enter. A PROFILE built on rapp-twin-chat §18. 'v' = swarm/kited-capable; drop the v and it runs fully offline byte-identically.
registry.when_to_use: When you want your OWN private, brandable, sealed closed room (own focus/kinds/rules/accent) — fork the template, edit neighborhood.json, ship Pages. Reach for it INSTEAD of rapp-commons when you need a closed room, not the open global square.
registry.entry_point: Use template -> Settings>Pages, edit neighborhood.json. Live: https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-vneighborhood/. CAVEAT: its PIN-seal is a front-door variant, NOT canonical rapp-sealed/1.0.

registry.spec_id: (none; Mirror-Spec instance, kernel byte-identical to grail)
registry.purpose: A single planted RAPP front door on the public internet — a deployed personal estate/instance ('real estate, not software'), kernel-compliant and byte-identical to the grail kernel (v0.6.0). Conforms to the Mirror Spec; rapp_brainstem/ files are kernel-locked, agents/+soul+UI are operator's choice.
registry.when_to_use: When you want a concrete public reference of the plant/mirror pattern — visit it, install ITS brainstem (a thin wrapper re-fetching the canonical grail installer so it can't drift), or hatch it as a twin. A worked example, not a protocol.
registry.entry_point: Visit https://kody-w.github.io/heimdall; install: curl -fsSL https://kody-w.github.io/heimdall/installer/install.sh | bash; hatch via twin-egg-hatcher --source kody-w/heimdall.

registry.purpose: The permanent cloud host (Azure Function) for kited vneighborhoods — an always-on 'resident vTwin' serving the same signed rapp-commons-event/1.0 stream over plain HTTP for any room, never sleeping. Trustless: verifies every POST server-side (ECDSA P-256, fingerprint match, bounded body); can refuse but cannot forge.
registry.when_to_use: When a browser-tab host isn't durable enough and you want a vneighborhood (the Commons, a forum, your room) to 'graduate' to an always-on relay so late joiners still get in. 'Kited is the floor, this is the ceiling.'
registry.entry_point: az login && func --version (v4) && ./deploy.sh; add base URL to neighborhood.json -> commons.cloud_hosts. Routes: /api/health, /api/rooms/{room}/events. Live: rapp-resident-kw165843.azurewebsites.net/api.

registry.purpose: The planetary swarm wire: drive far/intermittent brainstem EDGES as one swarm over append-only frames/echos served from GitHub. The edge is a brainstem making local judgments; verify-before-act; degrade-to-one offline.
registry.when_to_use: a brainstem on a spotty/high-latency link must act autonomously and re-aim when it reaches the swarm
registry.entry_point: drop edge_node_agent.py into a brainstem; raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rapp-frame-net/main/net/latest.json

registry.purpose: The many-headed unkillable medium: the swarm's static data is mirrored to many independent heads (GitHub raw, jsDelivr, raw.githack, mirrors, IPFS); read from any head, trust the content-hash not the host. Cut one head, two grow.
registry.when_to_use: I must keep reading the swarm even if a host/CDN is taken down, geoblocked, or bans the account
registry.entry_point: an edge head-list with content-verify + failover; HYDRA.md

registry.role: Canonical public catalog + spec for RAPP senses (modular per-channel output overlays that drop into a brainstem's utils/senses/); peer to RAR (agents) and RAPP_Store (rapplications)
registry.entry_point: Browse index.json / submit via @rapp/rapp_publish_agent or a [SENSE] @<user>/<slug> issue; install sense .py files into rapp_brainstem/utils/senses/

registry.role: Public catalog + canonical spec for rapplications (bundled single-file agent + UI/state/service/twin cartridges); defines bundle layout, manifest schema, gated-rapp federation, and the static Pokedex API.
registry.entry_point: Browse the static Pokedex API at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/RAPP_Store/main/api/v1/index.json (or the viewer kody-w.github.io/RAPP_Store/); rapps conform to rapp-application/1.0 and drop into a local brainstem.

registry.role: Canonical owner of the portable RAPPcards trading-card standard (card data model, deterministic seed identity, incantation protocol, lossless export, cross-binder federation) — declares rappcards-binder/1.0 + rappcards-peers/1.0 + rappcards-seed-index/1.0
registry.entry_point: Read SPEC.md for the standard; live site at kody-w.github.io/RAPPcards (index.html browse, binder.html local-first IndexedDB collection). Federation bootstrap: raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/RAPPcards/main/peers.json

registry.role: Owner of the rapp-demo/1.0 scenario schema and its scan-to-watch synced demo player: an M365-style template where a host walks a text scenario step-by-step and QR/link watchers follow live, read-only, over a rapp-sealed WebRTC channel.
registry.entry_point: Live player at https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-demos/ ; author/import a `rapp-demo/1.0` scenario JSON (see scenarios/) into the fixed M365 template and drive it; schema canonically defined inline in README.md

registry.role: The situational router/index over the full RAPP protocol stack — maps situation→layer→protocol across ~60 repos and ~33 specs; also owns FOUNDATION (foundation.json, the locked content-addressed canonical registry) and the crawlable registry.json
registry.entry_point: Crawl it: fetch https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rapp-spine/main/registry.json and match your situation against `router`, or run `python crawl.py "<situation>"`; full instructions in CRAWL.md. Read SPINE.md for the whole column + collisions.

registry.role: rapp-eternity/1.0 — The Rappid Identity & Ownership Standard
registry.entry_point: read SPEC.md in kody-w/rapp-eternity
read FLEET_CHAT.md in kody-w/leviathan
read specs/KERNEL.md in kody-w/rapp-spine
read METROPOLIS.md in kody-w/rapp-estate
read specs/AUTH_CHAIN.md in kody-w/rapp-spine

registry.entry_point: read specs/NETWORK_TRUST_BOUNDARY.md in kody-w/rapp-spine

registry.entry_point: read specs/TRUST_AND_OWNERSHIP.md in kody-w/rapp-spine
read specs/SUBSTRATE_TRUST.md in kody-w/rapp-spine
read specs/CUBBY.md in kody-w/rapp-spine

registry.role: The `.egg` Cartridge Family & Multi-Scale Hatch Contract
registry.entry_point: read EGG_FAMILY.md in kody-w/rapp-egg-hub
read PAYPHONE.md in kody-w/rapp-neighborhood-protocol
read specs/PARITY.md in kody-w/rapp-spine

registry.role: <!-- (c) 2026 Kody Wildfeuer · part of the RAPP ecosystem (RAPP) -->
registry.entry_point: read specs/CANON.md in kody-w/rapp-spine

collisions_and_gaps: LEVIATHAN — TWO unrelated concepts share the name (the load-bearing collision). (A) Leviathan PROTOCOL (kody-w/leviathan, self-titled SPEC v1.0): ONE external mind drives MANY no-LLM brainstem BODIES over POST /api/agent/<name> — a LAN FLEET/fan-out orchestration; 'one mind, many bodies.' (B) Wrapped-Organism Leviathan (rapp_leviathan_factory -> wrap_leviathan -> rapp-leviathan-hub/rapp-leviathan-egg/1.0): ONE operator's single digital BEING built of MANY CELLS across 5 estates (Sanctum/Polity/Works/Press/Commons); 'one being, many cells.' Same word, orthogonal architectures — a crawler must disambiguate by intent (drive a node fleet vs. mint/ship a being).

PORT + ROOT COLLISION: kody-w/RAPP, kody-w/rapp-installer, AND kody-w/rappterbox all install to ~/.brainstem and bind 127.0.0.1:7071. The three cannot coexist on one machine; CommunityRAPP also lands on 7071 locally (or 7072 scaffolded). Project-local RAPP uses :7072+.

DEPRECATIONS: kody-w/rapp-installer is SUPERSEDED by kody-w/RAPP (which folded the same three tiers into installer/ + rapp_swarm/ + worker/) but still self-identifies as 'RAPP Brainstem'. kody-w/RAPP_Hub is ARCHIVED and redirects to kody-w/rapphub. Treat installer as legacy/teaching, RAPP_Hub as dead.

MISSING / 404 PROTOCOLS (gaps): (1) The Wrapped Organism Spec — advertised home kody-w/rappter is 404, no version slug; only a reference runtime survives at kody-w/rappterbook/scripts/wrapped_organism/. (2) kody-w/wrap_leviathan ships NO code anywhere reachable — its wiring stage is aspirational; leviathan_hub `hatch` covers it today. (3) kody-w/rapp_leviathan_factory has no standalone repo (404) — the canonical artifact is a RAR agent (v0.2.0).

SEALING COLLISION: rapp-sealed/1.0 is the CANONICAL codec (PBKDF2-SHA256 salt 'rapp-neighborhood-5a/1', 210000 iters -> AES-256-GCM). rapp-vneighborhood/1.0 uses a DIFFERENT front-door PIN-seal variant (PBKDF2-SHA256(PIN, salt 'rapp-vneighborhood:'+channel, 100000) -> AES-GCM). Two incompatible schemes; never assume a vneighborhood channel is rapp-sealed bytes.

ALIAS, NOT A PROTOCOL: 'rapp-twin-chat/1.0' is an ALIAS for §6 of rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0, not a standalone spec. rapp-commons-protocol/2.0 and rapp-vneighborhood/1.0 are PROFILES/APPS on top of it; MCP is just another §5 transport carrying the same §6 envelope. None of these are new peer types.

'rappter*' NAMING CLUSTER: openrappter (divergent dual Python+TS runtime, data root ~/.openrappter, own hub rappterhub), rappterbox (console re-skin on ~/.brainstem), rappter (404, Wrapped Organism Spec home), and rappterbook (+~25 rappterbook-*/rappter-* repos) are DISTINCT things. openrappter shares only the Copilot-backed single-file thesis with the RAPP kernel — do not treat it as a RAPP runtime variant.

'kite' NAMING CLUSTER: rapp-kite (the operator 'string' — CDP tooling) is distinct from kite-mark (visual identity repo) and rapp-kited-twin (canonical kite-mark SVG). Three 'kite' things; only rapp-kite is the transport operator.

RAPPID IDENTITY REPRESENTATIONAL SEAMS: multiple on-the-wire forms coexist — rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex> (rapp-egg-hub v2.0), rappid:@owner/slug:<64hex> (rapp-eternity/1.0 canonical), rappid:v2:<kind>:@owner/slug:<hex> (rapp-estate), rappid:v3:<base64url(SHA-256(pub))> (rapp-commons). rapp-commons' event SCHEMA and its PROTOCOL.md even show different forms. Rule (per the compatibility contract): READ all legacy forms forever, EMIT only canonical, join on the hash — never rewrite identity in place.

ETERNITY IDENTITY LIVES IN THE WRONG LAYER: rapp-eternity/1.0 (the rappid:@owner/slug:64hex identity STANDARD) is canonically housed in RAPP-Bible (the map/aggregation layer), not the identity layer — an organizational seam a crawler should know about so it doesn't look for it under rapp-sealed/rapp-moment.

'DRIFT TRIANGLE' IS A MISNOMER — it has FOUR legs (the @rapp/rapp agent, rapp-god, rapp-map, RAPP-Bible) anchored on ecosystem-spec.json; when any leg disagrees with the JSON, the JSON wins. rapp-map and rapp-god both carry byte-identical mirrors of ecosystem-spec.json, so divergence between them IS the detectable drift signal.

OVERLAP — distribution catalogs: rapp-agents (flat public agent stack + RappLoader) overlaps RAR (curated content-addressed registry); rapp-egg-hub (twin .egg hub) overlaps twin-egg-hatcher (generic hatcher) and rapp-carts (the unifying cartridge abstraction). They are complementary (stack vs registry; hub-distribution vs hatch-tool vs user-facing unit), not duplicates — but a crawler should pick by role, not by nearest match.

OVERLAP — map renderers: RAPP-Bible (synthesized, PINNED v1.0.0 human narrative — can itself drift), rapp_docs (live raw passthrough, always-current, zero copies), and rapp-map (index of WHERE, not the TEXT) cover overlapping ground with different jobs. Use rapp_docs for current spec text, Bible for narrative onboarding, rapp-map for location.

LICENSE COLLISION: most of the cluster is MIT (rapp-sealed, rapp-dataverse, rapp-neighborhood-protocol, rapp-kite, rapp-static-apis, rapp-agents). BUT kody-w/RAPP and rappterbox are source-available All-Rights-Reserved, and rapp-moment/1.0 is PolyForm-Noncommercial 1.0.0 + trademarked marks ('RAPP', 'Holographic Moment(s)'). A consumer/redistributor must check per-repo terms, not assume MIT.

SECURITY GAP: the Leviathan fleet protocol's POST /api/agent/<name> route is UNAUTHENTICATED — a shell-capable agent on any body = fleet-wide RCE from one request. It is explicitly LAN/trusted-subnet only; there is no auth layer in the spec. Also the fleet protocol declares NO rapp-xxx/N.N slug, so it sits outside the registry's naming convention and won't show up in spec-id scans.

SPINE GAP: the rapp-spine itself has no formal spec id, version, or canonical home repo — it is a synthesis/router over existing specs (this document). It should likely be minted (e.g. rapp-spine/1.0) and wired into rapp-map/rapp-god as a first-class leg if it is to avoid becoming its own drift source.
